ask: Soften filter 9 from 'close<Fast EMA' to entry-on-rejection-alone — backtest shows 2 home-run trades ($704 + $606) blocked by current strict rule
decision_note: Live 12:30 ET miss of textbook 735.40 rejection triggered same-day relaxation that went further than R-BT-01 — dropped both close<Fast EMA and body<40% sub-clauses. Filter 9 now: red bar + 1.3x vol. Synced in heartbeat.md + filters.py. v3 backtest result confirms +$309 P&L vs prior strict version's negative.

ask: Widen breakeven stop to BE-3c to avoid theta-drift stop-outs on runner
decision_note: Awaiting paper-trade observations on runner BE-stop frequency before changing.

ask: Test size-up modifier in HIGH IV regime (VIX>22) — both home-runs fired here
decision_note: Defer until R-BT-01 settled and 20+ paper trades collected. Size modifier on top of unproven base = 2 unknowns.

ask: Polygon paid tier (~$30/mo) for >60-day history + tighter option-chain replays
decision_note: yfinance 60-day window sufficient for current n. Reconsider once paper-trade graduates to live deployment.

ask: DO NOT promote candlestick patterns (marubozu/shooting_star/hammer/engulfing) to filter 10 triggers
decision_note: v4 backtest (candles as triggers): 17 trades, 47% WR, $8 expectancy, +$135 P&L. v5 (awareness only): 13 trades, 54% WR, $24 expectancy, +$309 P&L. -56% P&L / -67% expectancy from adding the noise. Candlesticks are awareness language for chart description; NOT a trigger source. Codified in CLAUDE.md operating principle 6 + chart-anatomy.md.

ask: Promote --real-fills mode to canonical backtest pricing — Black-Scholes with VIX/100 IV proxy systematically under-prices winners and over-prices losers
decision_note: v5 (BS) vs v6 (real OPRA) on identical 13 trades: WR 54%→69%, P&L $309→$891 (+188%), W/L 1.29x→1.72x (now passes), max DD $-243→$-124. BS-mode remains for fast iteration with no fetch dependency, but expectancy/P&L from BS should be treated as conservative lower bounds. EOD-summary daily-backtest-sync should prefer real-fills when fetch budget allows.

ask: Live-deployment threshold 'trade count >= 20' is the only remaining failure under v6 — collect 7 more paper trades, then re-evaluate
decision_note: Under v6 real-fills, scorecard is WR 69% PASS, W/L 1.72x PASS, expectancy $69 PASS — only count fails. At ~1 paper trade per 3 trading days, n=20 takes ~60 trading days from start. Re-run weekly review when count crosses 20.

ask: SUPERSEDE: v6 had look-ahead bug. Promote v7 (next-bar entry + slippage) as canonical real-fill mode
decision_note: v6 used trigger-bar VWAP as entry — VWAP averages prices BEFORE trigger fires at bar close. This was look-ahead. v7 fixes by using next-bar open + $0.02 entry slippage. Walked 4/23 708P trade by hand: v6 entry $1.02 vs honest $1.58. v6 numbers SUPERSEDED. v7 is the truth: WR 46%, expectancy -$28, 1/4 PASS.

ask: REVISED: Under v7 honest fills, scorecard is 1/4 PASS not 3/4. DO NOT graduate engine to live money. Continue paper, tune the engine first
decision_note: v6 said 'only sample size fails.' v7 says WR barely passes, W/L 0.72x fails, expectancy -$28 fails, count fails. Engine fires on right days but timing/strike differ from J's discretion. Recommended hybrid mode: engine surfaces, J approves each entry.

ask: Engine fires later than J's manual reads — investigate earlier-bar entry preference. 4/29 J entered 10:25, engine fired 12:35; 5/4 J entered 10:27, engine fired 11:20
decision_note: With filter 9 already relaxed, what other filters delay first valid trigger of the day? Run sensitivity test: replay J's 3 historical days, compare engine entry bar to J's actual entry bar. If gap >2 bars consistently, identify the filter causing the delay.

ask: Test ITM-1 strike selection vs ATM-round. J consistently picks $1-2 ITM (closer to spot than round number). Engine picks ATM round-to-$1
decision_note: 4/29: J=710P, engine=709P. 5/4: J=721P, engine=719P. ITM strikes have higher delta (faster premium response to favorable moves) but more $ at risk. Re-run v7 with strike = round(spot - 1.0) for puts and round(spot + 1.0) for calls.

ask: Add best-of-day setup gating — wait until 11:00 ET, take highest-conviction setup of the day instead of first valid trigger
decision_note: v7 has 5 MIDDAY/AFTERNOON entries that mostly lose. Many of these are second-chance triggers J would skip. A 'wait for setup of the day' rule may improve avg quality at cost of trade count. Test: re-run v7 only allowing OPEN_DRIVE+MORNING entries.

ask: Add chart-level take-profit. TP1 should fire when SPY hits a documented support/resistance level, NOT only when premium hits +30%
decision_note: On 5/4, J's TP1 fired at $1.51 (+78% premium) when SPY hit 717.5 support. Engine's +30% TP1 would have fired at $1.105 — capping J's $738 trade at ~$200. The playbook v1.0 ALREADY says 'TP1 = +30% premium OR first major support, whichever first' — engine never implemented the chart-level half. Add: when position open, if SPY price reaches ANY level in key-levels.json with type=support (for puts) or resistance (for calls) BEFORE premium-% target, fire TP1 at price-based exit.

ask: Quantity sizing rule needs review. J trades 6-20 contracts; engine fixed at 3
decision_note: 4/29 J=6, 5/1 J=20, 5/4 J=10. Engine hardcodes qty=3 (DEFAULT_QTY in simulator). Risk-rules.md says 50% per-trade cap, min 3 contracts (2 TP + 1 runner), scale to 4 at $2K. But J trading $100K paper with 10-20 contracts is way more aggressive than the 3-contract floor. Either tighten the rule (good) or scale-up so engine matches J's effective risk per trade.

ask: Switch from autonomous-execution to setup-surfacer-with-J-approval
decision_note: Engine vs J on 6 days: J=+$920, engine=-$233. Engine correctly skips J's losers (5/5 -260, 5/6 -300) but misses J's winners (4/29 +352, 5/1 +510, 5/4 +738). Until R-BT-08/09/11 close the timing/strike/TP1 gaps, the engine should NOT autopilot. Best mode for tomorrow: heartbeat detects setup, writes ALERT to dashboard-dialogue, J pulls trigger manually within his existing broker workflow. Reuse the system as a setup-finder.

decision_note: Downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM: no A/B scorecard yet. Generate analysis/recommendations/R-0002.json before re-issuing as HIGH.

ask: Stop calculation uses limit price not fill price — June 18: limit 2.04, fill 1.83, stop 1.94. Since 1.83 < 1.94 stop, the position was below stop ON FILL, causing immediate 11:04 stop trigger and rest-of-day first_entry_lock. Fix: compute stop as (fill_price * (1 - stop_pct)) not (limit_price * (1 - stop_pct)).

Hypothesis-grades.jsonl NOT being populated from daily-review JSON predictions. June 16 daily review has 3 graded predictions but 0 rows appear in hypothesis-grades.jsonl for the week. Section 1 (hypothesis hit rate) blind for 3+ consecutive weeks. Fix: EOD pipeline must append each prediction row from daily-review JSON to hypothesis-grades.jsonl.
